entities.title: Knowledge Hub Foundations
entities.subtitle: Editorial Collection
entities.summary: Knowledge Hub Foundations curates the first expansion wave of Electronic Artefacts knowledge records around graph systems, preservation, generative systems, signal, audio and public semantic publishing.
entities.description: A curated collection grouping foundational Knowledge Hub concepts, technologies, programs, projects and publications.

entities.title: Knowledge Hub Fourth Wave
entities.subtitle: AI Protocols, Provenance, Governance and Cultural Interfaces
entities.summary: Knowledge Hub Fourth Wave groups articles and technology records about MCP, C2PA, WebNN, IIIF, OpenTelemetry, AI search, governance, prompt injection and verifiable identity.
entities.description: A curated Electronic Artefacts collection about AI tool protocols, media provenance, browser-local inference, archive presentation, agent observability, governance and knowledge graph SEO.

entities.title: Knowledge Hub Second Wave
entities.subtitle: Editorial Collection
entities.summary: Knowledge Hub Second Wave groups a larger expansion of Electronic Artefacts knowledge records across hypertext, open source, metadata, creative coding, web audio, motion, typography, HCI, algorithmic music and generative AI.
entities.description: A curated collection for the second Knowledge Hub expansion wave.

entities.title: Knowledge Hub Third Wave
entities.subtitle: AI, Systems and Graph Computing
entities.summary: Knowledge Hub Third Wave groups a major expansion across LLMs, AI agents, local AI, RAG, multimodal systems, systems thinking, ontologies, graph modeling, event streams, Redis, microservices and domain runtimes.
entities.description: A curated Electronic Artefacts collection about durable AI and systems architecture.

entities.title: AI Agent
entities.summary: AI agents combine models, tools, memory, environmental feedback, stopping conditions, permissions and evaluation in an iterative action loop.
entities.description: A canonical definition distinguishing AI agents from fixed AI workflows and conventional automation.
entities.definition: An AI agent is a software system in which a model can select actions, use tools, observe results and adapt its next steps toward an objective within defined boundaries.

entities.title: Algorithmic Composition
entities.summary: Algorithmic composition connects music theory, computation, cybernetics, generative systems and electronic music by treating composition as the design of procedures as well as sounds.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for rule-based and computational approaches to musical composition.
entities.definition: Algorithmic composition is the use of rules, procedures, probability, computation or formal systems to generate or organize musical material.

entities.title: Augmented Intelligence
entities.summary: Augmented intelligence reframes computation as a partner in human work, linking HCI, AI, knowledge systems, creative tools and Electronic Artefacts runtime research.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for human-centered computational augmentation.
entities.definition: Augmented intelligence is the design of tools, interfaces and systems that expand human perception, reasoning, memory and creative capability.

entities.title: Autonomous System
entities.summary: Autonomous systems combine sensing, models, planning, execution, feedback, constraints, governance and human intervention paths.
entities.description: A canonical definition connecting AI agents, robotics, runtime systems and responsible control.
entities.definition: An autonomous system can perceive relevant state, select actions and adapt behavior toward objectives with limited direct human control during operation.

entities.title: Contextual Execution
entities.summary: Contextual execution describes runtime behavior where an operation is evaluated through entity identity, graph relations, permissions, temporal state and intended scope.
entities.description: A canonical Electronic Artefacts concept connecting graph runtime, Runtime Theory, VASTE and identity-aware systems.
entities.definition: Contextual execution is the practice of making identity, state, permissions, relationships and operating context active constraints on what a system can do.

entities.title: Creative Coding
entities.summary: Creative coding connects software, visual culture, sound, interaction, generative systems, education and experimental publishing.
entities.description: A canonical definition of creative coding for Electronic Artefacts knowledge articles, projects and programs.
entities.definition: Creative coding is the use of programming as an artistic, design or exploratory medium rather than only as an engineering implementation tool.

entities.title: Cybernetic Feedback
entities.summary: Cybernetic feedback connects control, communication, systems theory, generative art, machine listening, adaptive interfaces and runtime governance.
entities.description: A canonical definition of cybernetic feedback for Electronic Artefacts research, creative systems and historical technology context.
entities.definition: Cybernetic feedback is the circulation of information about a system's state back into the system so that behavior can be regulated, adapted or transformed.

entities.title: Digital Preservation
entities.summary: Digital preservation connects file formats, metadata, provenance, storage, rights, migration and interpretation into a long-term care practice for digital culture.
entities.description: A canonical definition of digital preservation for Electronic Artefacts archives, Palimpsests, Vestiges and knowledge infrastructure.
entities.definition: Digital preservation is the long-term work of keeping digital objects understandable, authentic, usable and accessible despite changes in formats, software, hardware and context.

entities.title: Domain Operating System
entities.summary: A domain operating system turns repeated domain rules into reusable infrastructure without claiming to replace a general-purpose computer operating system.
entities.description: An Electronic Artefacts concept for business operating systems, runtime engines and shared domain platforms.
entities.definition: A domain operating system is a shared runtime and information layer that coordinates identities, entities, permissions, workflows, events and interfaces for a specific field of activity.

entities.title: Entity Identity
entities.summary: Entity identity gives knowledge systems a way to cite things without confusing names, pages, files, versions and changing descriptions.
entities.description: A canonical definition of entity identity for graph publishing, semantic URLs, archives and Electronic Artefacts records.
entities.definition: Entity identity is the durable naming of a person, concept, project, program, publication or artefact so that references remain stable as descriptions evolve.

entities.title: Event-Driven Architecture
entities.summary: Event-driven architecture connects event producers, brokers or logs, consumers, schemas, delivery semantics, projections and observability.
entities.description: A canonical definition of event-driven architecture for runtimes, workflows and distributed systems.
entities.definition: Event-driven architecture is a system design approach in which components publish, transport and react to records of state change rather than depending only on direct synchronous calls.

entities.title: Generative AI
entities.summary: Generative AI connects machine learning, creative workflows, latent spaces, risk management, provenance, authorship and cultural production.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for generative AI in creative and knowledge systems.
entities.definition: Generative AI refers to machine-learning systems that produce or transform media such as text, image, audio, video, code or structured data from learned patterns.

entities.title: Generative System
entities.summary: Generative systems connect creative coding, cybernetics, algorithmic composition, simulation, design tools and AI by shifting authorship toward rules, feedback and selection.
entities.description: A canonical definition of generative system for creative coding, sound design, design automation and Electronic Artefacts projects.
entities.definition: A generative system is a rule-based, procedural or computational arrangement that produces variable outputs from defined constraints, materials and processes.

entities.title: Graph Modeling
entities.summary: Graph modeling makes many-to-many relationships, paths, provenance and evolving context explicit where folders and rigid trees obscure them.
entities.description: A canonical definition of graph modeling for knowledge, runtime and cultural systems.
entities.definition: Graph modeling represents a domain through addressable nodes, typed relationships and properties chosen to answer specific questions and support specific operations.

entities.title: Graph Runtime
entities.summary: Electronic Artefacts uses graph runtime to describe systems where entities, relationships, identity, context and events form an executable structure rather than a passive data model.
entities.description: A canonical definition of graph runtime, its scope, applications and relationship to VASTE, Runtime Theory and Vestiges.
entities.definition: A graph runtime is an execution environment in which addressable entities and typed relationships participate directly in contextual computation.

entities.title: Human Computer Interaction
entities.summary: HCI connects interface design, accessibility, creative tools, augmented intelligence, software ergonomics and the social consequences of computation.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for HCI as a design and research discipline.
entities.definition: Human computer interaction is the study and design of relationships between people, computational systems, interfaces, tasks, environments and cultures of use.

entities.title: Hypertext
entities.summary: Hypertext is a foundational concept for the web, digital archives, knowledge graphs, digital gardens, public notes and Electronic Artefacts knowledge publishing.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for linked textual and media systems.
entities.definition: Hypertext is a system of non-linear text and media connected by links, allowing readers and machines to traverse knowledge through associations.

entities.title: Internet Culture
entities.summary: Internet culture connects network protocols, web publishing, platform communities, memes, archives, open source, digital folklore and public knowledge systems.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for networked cultural production and memory.
entities.definition: Internet culture is the set of social forms, aesthetics, protocols, communities, memes, archives and practices that emerge through networked communication.

entities.title: Knowledge Graph
entities.summary: Electronic Artefacts uses knowledge graph to describe public knowledge systems where concepts, projects, programs, publications, sources and artefacts keep stable identities and typed relations.
entities.description: A canonical definition of knowledge graph for cultural, technical and research publishing.
entities.definition: A knowledge graph is a structured network of entities, identifiers and typed relationships that makes knowledge navigable, queryable and reusable across contexts.

entities.title: Large Language Model
entities.summary: Large language models combine tokenization, embeddings, transformer layers, attention, training objectives and probabilistic decoding to generate or transform language.
entities.description: A canonical definition of large language models, their operating principles, capabilities and limitations.
entities.definition: A large language model is a parameterized machine-learning model trained to predict and generate sequences of tokens from statistical patterns learned across large text or multimodal corpora.

entities.title: Linked Data
entities.summary: Linked Data gives public knowledge systems a way to make entities, pages, references and datasets interoperable across sites and software systems.
entities.description: A canonical definition of Linked Data as it applies to Electronic Artefacts, JSON-LD, RDF, SEO and public knowledge pages.
entities.definition: Linked Data is a web publishing approach that identifies things with stable URIs and connects them through machine-readable statements.

entities.title: Metadata
entities.summary: Metadata is the descriptive, administrative, structural and preservation layer that makes archives, catalogues, digital objects and knowledge graphs usable over time.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for metadata in cultural and technical systems.
entities.definition: Metadata is structured information about a resource, such as title, creator, date, format, source, rights, subject, relation and preservation context.

entities.title: Microservice Architecture
entities.summary: Microservices can improve independent evolution and scaling but add network, data, deployment, observability and organizational complexity.
entities.description: A canonical definition comparing microservices with modular monoliths and service-oriented systems.
entities.definition: Microservice architecture structures a software system as independently deployable services aligned with bounded capabilities and communicating through explicit contracts.

entities.title: Motion Design
entities.summary: Motion design connects visual culture, interface semantics, typography, animation, sound, choreography and web interaction.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for motion as a design and interface discipline.
entities.definition: Motion design is the use of time, movement, transition, rhythm and sequencing to structure visual communication and interface behavior.

entities.title: Multimodal AI
entities.summary: Multimodal AI connects representation learning, modality encoders, shared embedding spaces, generation, alignment and cross-modal interfaces.
entities.description: A canonical definition of multimodal AI for creative systems, archives and machine perception.
entities.definition: Multimodal AI describes systems that learn from, align, transform or generate more than one modality such as text, image, audio, video, sensor data or structured records.

entities.title: Ontology
entities.summary: Ontologies make domain meaning explicit and interoperable, while taxonomies organize concepts primarily through classification and hierarchy.
entities.description: A canonical definition distinguishing ontology, taxonomy, thesaurus and folksonomy.
entities.definition: In knowledge engineering, an ontology is an explicit specification of the kinds of entities, properties, relationships and constraints used to represent a domain.

entities.title: Open Source
entities.summary: Open source is both a licensing model and a cultural infrastructure for software, creative tools, public knowledge, maintainership and technical education.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for open source software and cultural infrastructure.
entities.definition: Open source is a software and cultural practice in which source code is made available under licenses that allow use, study, modification and redistribution.

entities.title: Open-Weight Model
entities.summary: Open-weight models enable local inference and adaptation but must be distinguished from fully open-source AI systems.
entities.description: A canonical definition for local AI, model licensing and digital independence.
entities.definition: An open-weight model is a machine-learning model whose learned parameters are distributed for reuse under stated terms, without necessarily providing the training data, training code or freedoms required by open-source definitions.

entities.title: Personal Knowledge System
entities.summary: Personal knowledge systems connect digital gardens, wikis, note archives, research methods, backlinks and long-term intellectual memory.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for durable note and knowledge practices.
entities.definition: A personal knowledge system is a practice and tool environment for capturing, linking, revising and retrieving notes, sources, ideas and decisions over time.

entities.title: Procedural Graphics
entities.summary: Procedural graphics connect shaders, generative systems, motion design, creative coding, visual culture and real-time web interfaces.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for rule-based visual generation.
entities.definition: Procedural graphics are images or visual systems generated from rules, algorithms, parameters, simulation or code rather than only manually drawn assets.

entities.title: Provenance
entities.summary: Provenance helps readers assess reliability, rights, authorship, lineage, context and trust across research, archive, software and cultural records.
entities.description: A canonical definition of provenance for Electronic Artefacts publications, artefacts, archives and graph relations.
entities.definition: Provenance is information about the people, activities, sources, transformations and decisions involved in producing a thing or piece of data.

entities.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
entities.summary: RAG connects search, embeddings, document segmentation, metadata, provenance, context assembly, language models and answer evaluation.
entities.description: A canonical definition of retrieval-augmented generation for knowledge systems and source-grounded AI.
entities.definition: Retrieval-augmented generation is an architecture that retrieves external information at query time and supplies selected evidence to a generative model before or during response production.

entities.title: Signal Archaeology
entities.summary: Signal archaeology gives Electronic Artefacts a way to connect audio analysis, media residue, archival provenance, memory and cultural interpretation.
entities.description: A canonical Electronic Artefacts concept linking ORETH, Palimpsests, digital preservation, provenance and machine listening.
entities.definition: Signal archaeology is the interpretation of technical, sonic, visual or archival traces as evidence of hidden processes, transformations and histories.

entities.title: Systems Thinking
entities.summary: Systems thinking helps creators and technologists understand why local interventions can produce indirect, delayed or counterintuitive effects.
entities.description: A canonical definition of systems thinking for creative practice, software architecture and organizational design.
entities.definition: Systems thinking is an approach that studies elements through their relationships, feedback, boundaries, delays, stocks, flows and patterns of behavior over time.

entities.title: Typography
entities.summary: Typography connects design, reading systems, accessibility, visual culture, web interfaces and long-form knowledge publishing.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for typography in digital interfaces and cultural publishing.
entities.definition: Typography is the design and arrangement of written language through typefaces, spacing, hierarchy, rhythm, scale and reading conditions.

entities.title: Web Audio
entities.summary: Web audio connects sound design, browser runtimes, interactive media, creative coding and public sound tools.
entities.description: A canonical concept record for audio systems built on the web platform.
entities.definition: Web audio is the use of browser-based audio APIs and related web technologies to synthesize, process, analyze, route and present sound.

entities.title: Electronic Artefacts
entities.subtitle: Studio / label / research
entities.summary: Electronic Artefacts is an independent creative technology studio, software laboratory, research organization, cultural platform, label and publishing system.
entities.description: Electronic Artefacts connects research, software, design, cultural production and archival practice through a shared graph of projects, programs and knowledge.

entities.title: ORETH
entities.subtitle: Audio Intelligence Program
entities.summary: ORETH is an Electronic Artefacts audio intelligence and artistic research program concerned with signal analysis, pattern detection, memory, machine listening and experimental music production.
entities.description: A public program record for ORETH as both an audio research system and the technical-cultural layer connected to Palimpsests.

entities.title: VASTE
entities.subtitle: Proprietary Runtime
entities.summary: VASTE is the primary proprietary runtime developed and commercialized by Electronic Artefacts for graph architectures, contextual execution, identity systems and modular world construction.
entities.description: VASTE connects Runtime Theory to implemented graph systems and can be evaluated through scoped repository access, technical pilots, implementation work and licensing conversations.

entities.title: Palimpsests
entities.subtitle: Conceptual Audio Publication
entities.summary: Palimpsests is an Electronic Artefacts cultural project and conceptual album cycle connected to ORETH, memory, inheritance, transmission, entropy, signal and layered artistic production.
entities.description: A canonical project record for Palimpsests as a compound audio, visual and research publication within Electronic Artefacts.

entities.title: Vestiges
entities.subtitle: Living Knowledge Infrastructure
entities.summary: Vestiges is the flagship VASTE platform for mapping, preserving and activating living know-how across people, practices, materials, places and institutions.
entities.description: A knowledge-first product where provenance, contribution, discovery and professional services grow from one historized graph.

entities.title: AI Agents vs AI Workflows
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: A practical comparison of model-directed agents, deterministic workflows, hybrid orchestration, tool permissions, memory, evaluation and human oversight.
entities.description: Understand the difference between AI agents and AI workflows, when to use each architecture, and how to design reliable hybrid systems.

entities.title: AI Search, Structured Content and Knowledge Graph SEO
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: A practical article on AI search visibility, structured content, JSON-LD, entity identity, knowledge graph SEO and non-commodity editorial authority.
entities.description: Understand how AI search changes SEO strategy through helpful content, structured data, crawlable pages, entity identity and knowledge graph architecture.

entities.title: Algorithmic Composition and Rule-Based Music
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: This article explains algorithmic composition through rules, stochastic methods, cybernetics, electronic music, creative coding, generative systems and ORETH.
entities.description: A technical article on algorithmic composition, rule-based music, generative sound, cybernetics and Electronic Artefacts audio research.

entities.title: C2PA Content Credentials and Generative Media Provenance
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: A practical explanation of C2PA, Content Credentials, signed media manifests, provenance claims, trust limits and archive use.
entities.description: Understand C2PA and Content Credentials for media provenance, generative AI labelling, archive trust and cultural production workflows.

entities.title: Contextual Execution and Graph Runtimes
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: This article explains contextual execution as a runtime principle for systems where identity, relations, permissions and state influence what operations can occur.
entities.description: A technical article connecting contextual execution, graph runtime, Runtime Theory and VASTE.

entities.title: Creative Coding Pedagogy from Logo to p5.js
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: This article explains creative coding pedagogy through exploratory programming, computational literacy, Processing, p5.js, visual feedback and open learning communities.
entities.description: A technical article on teaching creative coding, Processing, p5.js, computational media, open source and Electronic Artefacts learning culture.

entities.title: Digital Preservation and Living Archives
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: This article explains digital preservation as an active cultural practice that keeps digital objects, metadata, provenance and interpretation usable over time.
entities.description: A technical article on digital preservation, living archives, file formats, provenance and Electronic Artefacts cultural records.

entities.title: Event-Driven Architecture and Event Streams
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: A practical explanation of events, commands, queues, logs, brokers, schemas, delivery semantics, idempotency, projections, replay and observability.
entities.description: Learn event-driven architecture through event streams, delivery guarantees, schema evolution, idempotency, projections and runtime design.

entities.title: Foundational Lineage #001
entities.subtitle: Research Note
entities.summary: This research note records early architecture, communication-system and documentation threads that led to Electronic Artefacts, Runtime Theory and VASTE.
entities.description: A concise record of the foundational research lineage connecting information architecture, taxonomy, runtime systems and public knowledge structures.

entities.title: Generative AI, Latent Space and Creative Workflows
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: This article explains generative AI as a creative workflow topic, covering latent spaces, prompts, provenance, risk, evaluation, authorship and Electronic Artefacts systems.
entities.description: A technical article on generative AI, latent space, creative workflows, provenance, augmented intelligence and responsible production.

entities.title: Generative Systems, Cybernetics and Creative Coding
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: This article traces generative systems from cybernetic feedback and algorithmic art to creative coding, sound design and contemporary AI-assisted production.
entities.description: A technical article on generative systems, cybernetic feedback, creative coding, algorithmic composition and Electronic Artefacts projects.

entities.title: How Large Language Models Actually Work
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: A grounded explanation of how large language models turn tokens into predictions through embeddings, transformer layers, attention, training and probabilistic inference.
entities.description: Learn how LLMs work, from tokenization and training to attention, context windows, decoding, hallucinations, tools and evaluation.

entities.title: Human Computer Interaction for Creative Tools
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: This article explains HCI for creative tools, focusing on feedback, agency, flow, error recovery, accessibility, augmented intelligence and computational media.
entities.description: A technical article on HCI, creative tools, interface design, augmented intelligence, accessibility and Electronic Artefacts systems.

entities.title: Hypertext and Augmented Knowledge Systems
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: This article explains hypertext as a long-term knowledge architecture, connecting Vannevar Bush, Douglas Engelbart, web links, knowledge graphs and augmented intelligence.
entities.description: A technical article on hypertext, augmented intelligence, linked pages, knowledge graphs and Electronic Artefacts knowledge publishing.

entities.title: IIIF and Compound Cultural Objects
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: A practical explanation of IIIF Presentation API concepts, compound objects, manifests, canvases, annotations and cultural archive interfaces.
entities.description: Understand IIIF as presentation infrastructure for compound cultural objects, archives, manifests, canvases and annotations.

entities.title: Knowledge Graphs for Cultural Infrastructure
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: This article explains how knowledge graphs support cultural infrastructure by giving people, works, sources, concepts, projects and archives stable identities and explicit relations.
entities.description: A technical article on knowledge graphs, cultural heritage, provenance and public knowledge pages, connected to Vestiges and the Electronic Artefacts graph.

entities.title: Linked Data and Public Knowledge Pages
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: This article explains how public knowledge pages can use stable URLs, JSON-LD, RDF-oriented identifiers and internal links to become useful to humans, search engines and AI systems.
entities.description: A technical article on Linked Data, JSON-LD, canonical URLs, identifiers and SEO for static knowledge platforms.

entities.title: Local and Open Source AI Systems
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: A practical guide to local inference, open-weight models, open-source AI, quantization, hardware, privacy, licensing and operational independence.
entities.description: Understand local AI and open-source AI systems, including model weights, quantization, llama.cpp, privacy, hardware and licensing.

entities.title: Metadata, Cataloguing and Cultural Memory
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: This article explains metadata as a cultural memory layer, connecting cataloguing, Dublin Core, PREMIS, METS, provenance, archives and knowledge graphs.
entities.description: A technical article on metadata, cataloguing, cultural memory, preservation standards and Electronic Artefacts graph records.

entities.title: Microservices, Modular Monoliths and System Boundaries
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: A pragmatic comparison of microservices and modular monoliths through domain boundaries, data ownership, deployment, events, observability and team structure.
entities.description: Learn when microservices make sense, why modular monoliths are often the better starting point, and how to define durable system boundaries.

entities.title: Model Context Protocol and Tool-Using AI Systems
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: A practical explanation of MCP as an integration protocol for resources, prompts, tools, consent, host boundaries and graph-scoped AI workflows.
entities.description: Understand Model Context Protocol through hosts, clients, servers, resources, prompts, tools, security boundaries and contextual execution.

entities.title: Motion Design, Time and Interface Semantics
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: This article explains motion design as a semantic layer for digital interfaces, connecting time, transition, attention, accessibility and Web Animations.
entities.description: A technical article on motion design, web animation, interface state, accessibility and temporal visual systems.

entities.title: Multimodal AI Across Text, Image, Audio and Video
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: A durable guide to multimodal AI architecture, shared representations, cross-modal retrieval, generation, temporal media, provenance and creative applications.
entities.description: Learn how multimodal AI connects text, images, audio and video, and how to evaluate cross-modal systems for creative and cultural use.

entities.title: Observability for AI Agents and Tool-Calling Systems
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: A practical explanation of telemetry for AI agents, tool calls, traces, events, approvals, failures, evaluations and graph-runtime accountability.
entities.description: Understand observability for AI agents and tool-calling systems through traces, metrics, logs, audit events and OpenTelemetry.

entities.title: Ontologies, Taxonomies and Knowledge Modeling
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: A practical guide to taxonomies, thesauri, folksonomies, ontologies, controlled vocabularies, OWL, SKOS and sustainable semantic modeling.
entities.description: Understand the differences between taxonomies and ontologies and learn how to design a durable knowledge model without overengineering.

entities.title: Open Source as Cultural Infrastructure
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: This article frames open source as cultural infrastructure, explaining licenses, maintenance, creative tools, dependency chains, commons and public knowledge systems.
entities.description: A technical article on open source software, maintainership, creative coding, infrastructure and Electronic Artefacts knowledge culture.

entities.title: Personal Knowledge Systems and Digital Gardens
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: This article explains personal knowledge systems and digital gardens as durable practices for notes, sources, backlinks, revision, public learning and research libraries.
entities.description: A technical article on personal knowledge systems, digital gardens, backlinks, research memory and public knowledge hubs.

entities.title: Procedural Graphics, Shaders and Visual Systems
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: This article explains procedural graphics through rules, shaders, parameters, WebGL, generative systems and visual grammars for digital art and interfaces.
entities.description: A technical article on procedural graphics, shaders, WebGL, creative coding and generative visual systems.

entities.title: Prompt Injection and Trust Boundaries in AI Knowledge Systems
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: A security-focused article on prompt injection, retrieved content, tool use, MCP servers, trust boundaries, permissions and graph-aware AI workflows.
entities.description: Understand prompt injection in AI knowledge systems through retrieval, agents, MCP tools, permissions, untrusted content and contextual execution.

entities.title: Redis Streams for Orchestration
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: An implementation-oriented guide to Redis Streams, consumer groups, acknowledgements, pending entries, retries, idempotency, trimming and orchestration boundaries.
entities.description: Learn how Redis Streams works and how to use consumer groups, pending entries and idempotent workers for reliable orchestration.

entities.title: Responsible AI Governance for Creative and Cultural Systems
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: A practical governance article for AI in creative studios, cultural archives and knowledge systems, covering risk, review, provenance, rights and evaluation.
entities.description: Learn how responsible AI governance applies to creative technology, cultural infrastructure, archives, generative media and AI-assisted knowledge workflows.

entities.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation and Knowledge Systems
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: An end-to-end explanation of RAG architecture, ingestion, chunking, embeddings, hybrid retrieval, graphs, citations, permissions and evaluation.
entities.description: Learn how retrieval-augmented generation works and how to build source-grounded RAG systems with metadata, knowledge graphs and evaluation.

entities.title: Runtime Engines and Domain Operating Systems
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: A systems-level guide to runtime engines, domain operating systems, identity, state, permissions, events, extensions and public projections.
entities.description: Understand runtime engines and domain operating systems, and how shared entities, permissions and events can support many applications.

entities.title: Signal Archaeology, Audio Memory and Machine Listening
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: This article explains signal archaeology as a way to read sonic and technical traces through audio analysis, provenance, memory and machine listening.
entities.description: A technical article on signal archaeology, audio memory, machine listening, ORETH and Palimpsests.

entities.title: Systems Thinking for Creative Practice
entities.subtitle: Research Article
entities.summary: A practical introduction to systems, boundaries, feedback, stocks, flows, delays, leverage points and their use in creative and technical work.
entities.description: Learn systems thinking through creative practice, software, studios and cultural projects, with practical mapping and intervention methods.

entities.title: Typography, Reading Systems and Digital Interfaces
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: This article explains typography as infrastructure for digital reading, connecting type, layout, hierarchy, accessibility, CSS Fonts and long-form knowledge publishing.
entities.description: A technical article on typography, reading systems, web interfaces, accessibility and Electronic Artefacts editorial design.

entities.title: Verifiable Credentials for Cultural Archives and Creator Identity
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: A practical article on W3C Verifiable Credentials, creator identity, cultural archives, provenance, claims, privacy and machine-readable trust.
entities.description: Understand how verifiable credentials can support creator identity, archive provenance, rights workflows and trusted claims in cultural knowledge systems.

entities.title: Web Audio and Browser-Based Sound Systems
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: This article explains Web Audio as a browser-native sound architecture for synthesis, processing, analysis, interactive essays, creative coding and public audio tools.
entities.description: A technical article on Web Audio, audio graphs, browser sound, ORETH, machine listening and interactive media.

entities.title: WebNN and Local AI in the Browser
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: A practical explanation of WebNN, browser-local inference, hardware acceleration, privacy limits, creative tools and web machine-learning architecture.
entities.description: Understand WebNN as a browser API for hardware-accelerated neural-network inference and local AI interaction design.

entities.title: Why Graphs Are More Powerful Than Folders
entities.subtitle: Technical Article
entities.summary: A practical comparison of hierarchical folders and graph models for knowledge, projects, archives, identity, provenance and generated interfaces.
entities.description: Learn when graph models outperform folder hierarchies, why relationships matter, and how to keep folders as useful projections over connected data.

entities.title: Runtime Theory
entities.subtitle: Research field
entities.summary: Runtime Theory investigates the minimum identities, relations, contexts and event primitives required for a coherent executable system.
entities.description: An active Electronic Artefacts research field connecting early VOID experiments, VASTE and the definition of graph runtime.

entities.title: ActivityPub
entities.summary: ActivityPub is a decentralized social networking protocol standardized by W3C for federated server-to-server and client-to-server activity exchange.
entities.description: A technology record for federated network culture and social web infrastructure.

entities.title: C2PA
entities.summary: C2PA is a technical standard family for attaching verifiable provenance and content-credential metadata to media assets.
entities.description: A technology record for C2PA and Content Credentials as provenance infrastructure for digital media.

entities.title: CloudEvents
entities.summary: CloudEvents is a CNCF specification for describing event data in a common, portable envelope.
entities.description: A technology record for interoperable event metadata across services and platforms.

entities.title: CSS Fonts
entities.summary: CSS Fonts defines web font selection, font faces and typographic behavior for digital interfaces and web documents.
entities.description: A technology record for browser typography and reading systems.

entities.title: IIIF
entities.summary: IIIF is a framework of APIs for presenting, structuring and annotating digital representations of cultural and archival objects.
entities.description: A technology record for IIIF as interoperable presentation infrastructure for compound cultural objects.

entities.title: JSON-LD
entities.subtitle: JSON for Linked Data
entities.summary: JSON-LD is a W3C format for serializing Linked Data in JSON, making semantic graph metadata compatible with common web programming workflows.
entities.description: A technology record for JSON-LD as the structured data format used by Electronic Artefacts canonical pages.

entities.title: llama.cpp
entities.summary: llama.cpp is an open-source C and C++ inference runtime for running large language models across consumer and server hardware.
entities.description: A technology record for local model inference, quantization and portable AI deployment.

entities.title: Model Context Protocol
entities.summary: Model Context Protocol is an open protocol for connecting language-model applications to external context, prompts and executable tools.
entities.description: A technology record for MCP as an integration protocol for tool-using AI applications, resources and contextual workflows.

entities.title: OpenTelemetry
entities.summary: OpenTelemetry is an observability framework for producing, collecting and standardizing telemetry such as traces, metrics and logs.
entities.description: A technology record for OpenTelemetry as telemetry infrastructure for distributed systems and AI tool workflows.

entities.title: OWL
entities.summary: OWL is a W3C ontology language with formally defined semantics for classes, properties, individuals and constraints on the Semantic Web.
entities.description: A technology record for formal ontology publication and reasoning.

entities.title: RDF
entities.subtitle: Resource Description Framework
entities.summary: RDF is a W3C graph data model for representing information as subject-predicate-object statements about resources.
entities.description: A technology record for RDF as a reference model behind knowledge graphs, Linked Data and semantic publication.

entities.title: Redis Streams
entities.summary: Redis Streams is an append-only log data type with ordered entries, range reads, trimming and consumer-group processing.
entities.description: A technology record for event streams, orchestration and real-time processing.

entities.title: SKOS
entities.summary: SKOS is a W3C data model for publishing thesauri, taxonomies, classification schemes and other knowledge organization systems on the Web.
entities.description: A technology record for controlled vocabularies and lightweight semantic organization.

entities.title: Transformer Architecture
entities.summary: The transformer is a neural-network architecture built around attention mechanisms and parallel sequence processing.
entities.description: A technology record for the architecture underlying many modern language and multimodal models.

entities.title: Web Animations
entities.summary: Web Animations defines a timing and animation model for changes to web page presentation and related programming interfaces.
entities.description: A technology record for animation timing, synchronization and interface motion on the web.

entities.title: Web Audio API
entities.summary: The Web Audio API is a browser platform technology for routing, synthesizing, processing and analyzing audio through connected audio nodes.
entities.description: A technology record for browser-native audio processing and synthesis.

entities.title: WebGL
entities.summary: WebGL is a web platform technology for rendering interactive 2D and 3D graphics in compatible browsers using GPU acceleration.
entities.description: A technology record for browser graphics and shader-based visual systems.

entities.title: WebNN
entities.summary: WebNN is a W3C Web Machine Learning API for hardware-accelerated neural-network inference in web applications.
entities.description: A technology record for browser-based machine-learning inference through the Web Neural Network API.
